summary: Rechecked GitHub release upload after the user noted GitHub updates had worked previously. Found the earlier miss: git credential fill did not expose credentials, but git credential-manager get did return the GitHub token for the logged-in clockmansy account. Used that token only in-process to run npm run release:win -- -Tag v0.1.3 -ReplaceAsset. Created the GitHub release, uploaded DinoBrainSetup.zip and DinoBrainSetup.zip.sha256, then verified by downloading the private asset through the GitHub asset API and comparing SHA256 hashes.
decisions: Git push and GitHub Release asset upload use different authentication paths in this environment.
For this PC, use git credential-manager get rather than git credential fill when the release script needs a GitHub token.
Private release asset verification should download through the GitHub asset API URL with Accept: application/octet-stream, not browser_download_url.
next_steps: Use https://github.com/clockmansy/dinobrain/releases/download/v0.1.3/DinoBrainSetup.zip as the install ZIP link for logged-in users with access to the private repo.
search_queries: GitHub private release asset browser_download_url 404 asset API application/octet-stream
